sections.paragraph:
fut. יִפְעַל; once יִפְעֹל.; followed by Makk. יִפְעָל־ ( Job 35:6) i.q. עָשָׂה to make, to do, a word of great use in cognate languages [“rarely used in Arab.”] (ܦܥܠܰ, فعل); in Hebrew only used poetically. Job 11:8, מַה־תִּפְעַל “what wilt thou do?” Psa 11:3, עַדִּיק מַה־פָּעַל “what shall the righteous do?” Deu 32:27, לֹא יְהֹוָה פָּעַל כָּל־אֵלֶּה “has not Jehovah made all these things;” Job 33:29 Isa 43:13. Specially it is-(a) to make, to fabricate, e.g. an idol, Isa 44:15 to make (to dig) a pit, Psa 7:14. Absol. Isa 44:12, פָּעַל בַּפֶּחָם “he laboureth in the coals.”-(b) to produce, to create, Psa 74:12 whence פֹּעֲלִי my creator, Job 36:3.-(c) to prepare. Exo 15:17, “the place which thou hast prepared for dwelling.” Hence to attempt, to undertake any thing (opp. to עָשָׂה to effect). Isa 41:4, מִי פָעַל וְעָשָׂה “who hath attempted and done it” (compare Isa 43:7, יְצַרְתִּיו אַף עֲשִׂיתִיו)? Mic 2:1 Psa 58:3, “ye devise wickedness in your hearts” (A similar use is made of עָשָׂה Isa 32:6 37:26 ).-(d) to do (i.e. to exercise) justice, Psa 15:2 wickedness, Job 34:32 36:23 . פֹּעֲלֵי אָוֶן evil doers, wicked men, Psa 5:6 6:9 14:4 and frequently.-(e) Followed by an acc. and לְ of the thing, Psa 7:14, חִצָּיו לְדֹלְקִים יִפְעָל “he makes his arrows burning.”-(f) Followed by לְ of pers. to do any thing for any one, whether good, Job 22:17 Psa 31:20, or evil, Job 7:20 followed by בְּ id., 35:6.

Derived nouns, פֹּעַל, פְּעֻלָה, מִפְעָל.
